behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted ... and included in the vulnerability check." This is a module-handoff failure boundary (manifest parser/dependency graph -> vulnerability-check input), not a single pure function. Integration brake checks: no explicit need for real OS/network/subprocess, no AI/ML dependency, no multi-agentic chain. Rejected higher level: no behavior text requiring real package-manager subprocess or live registry/network resolution.
behaviors.test_description: Wire manifest parsing + dependency expansion + vulnerability-check request builder in-process (using fixture manifests with transitive deps) and assert the emitted check set includes every direct/transitive package/version exactly once.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "query returns results that match the exact version ranges ... not just the package name." This depends on real HTTP/API contract behavior for version-range filtering; mocks can hide server-side interpretation mismatches. Rejected higher level: no behavior text involving AI/ML output or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real vulnerability API endpoint (or real staging instance) with controlled package/version-range cases; assert returned CVEs differ when version ranges differ for the same package name and match expected affected ranges.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is pure ordering logic: "severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low ... same severity sorted by CVSS score." No module boundary, no real infra, no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the comparator/sort function with mixed severities and tied severities; assert exact order by severity rank then descending CVSS.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged." Real failure typically appears across parse->mutate->serialize modules (state preservation), not just one function. Integration brake checks: no explicit real OS/network/subprocess requirement, no AI/ML dependency, no multi-step agentic flow. Rejected higher level: no text requiring real repo/write watcher/OS semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Execute manifest read/parse/update/write pipeline in-process on fixture manifests and assert only target dependency version changes (including unchanged sibling deps/sections).

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "created on the real repository" and "correct single-dependency diff." This explicitly requires real VCS/remote API and real diff behavior; mocking would hide authentication, branch, and server-side PR creation failures. Rejected higher level: no AI/ML output dependency and no multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end against a real test repository: trigger scanner, verify PR exists remotely, and validate PR diff contains exactly one dependency version change and no unrelated file modifications.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is local deterministic rule evaluation: "major version bump ... identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison." No required I/O or cross-module boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test semver comparison helper with representative version pairs (major/minor/patch/prerelease) and assert only major increments are marked breaking per policy.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced" requires orchestration handoff across semver decision -> PR module -> reporting module. Integration brake checks: no explicit need for real OS/network if PR client is in-process fake, no AI/ML dependency, no multi-agentic chain. Rejected System: behavior does not require validating remote PR API behavior, only that creation is skipped and flag emitted.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scanner decision engine with semver checker, PR service interface (spy/fake), and output renderer; feed a breaking-bump case and assert PR service is not invoked and review flag appears in emitted scanner report.
